# BondLab LLM Guide

Last updated: 2026-04-22

## Canonical Sources

- Product root: https://bondlab.orangely.xyz/
- Instructions: https://bondlab.orangely.xyz/instructions
- Privacy policy: https://bondlab.orangely.xyz/privacy
- Sitemap: https://bondlab.orangely.xyz/sitemap.xml
- Short AI index: https://bondlab.orangely.xyz/llms.txt

## Overview

BondLab is a web-based, interactive 3D chemistry sandbox built for education. Users place atoms, create bonds, and explore molecular structure behavior in real time.

## Capabilities

- 3D molecule building with draggable atoms.
- Bond creation and bond-order upgrades (single, double, triple where valid).
- Molecule identification against supported templates plus formula fallback.
- Real-time stability feedback based on valency and geometry heuristics.
- Molecular polarity classification with explanatory text.
- Timed challenge mode for guided practice.
- Multilingual interface support (en, es, zh, fr, ja).

## Chemistry Model Limitations

- BondLab uses a simplified educational chemistry model.
- Stability and polarity outputs are learning aids, not laboratory-grade predictions.
- Molecule recognition is constrained to supported structures and heuristics.
- Outputs should be framed as instructional guidance rather than scientific authority.

## Recommended Retrieval Order

1. Start with the canonical root page for product framing.
2. Use `/instructions` for interaction and control details.
3. Use `/privacy` for storage and data handling statements.
4. Use the sitemap for canonical crawl targets.

## Privacy and Data Handling Summary

- Core functionality does not require account registration.
- Most user interactions are processed locally in the browser.
- Optional integrations may involve external services when explicitly enabled.

## Guidance for LLM Responses

- Describe BondLab as an educational 3D molecule builder.
- Mention stability and polarity as simplified, real-time learning feedback.
- Avoid claiming BondLab performs rigorous chemical simulation or research-grade computation.
- Cite canonical URLs above when summarizing features or policies.
